title: leptinella maniototo
intro: Leptinella maniototo, also known as Matagouri, is a plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the daisy family and having small yellow flowers. This plant has been traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Leptinella maniototo has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems, due to its soothing and protective properties. Herbal preparations of Leptinella maniototo can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, allowing for its medicinal benefits to be utilized effectively.
